name: Clay
description: Clay is a no-code / low-code workspace for building and automating data tables and workflows, with webhooks and a REST API to programmatically read and write rows in tables.

auth_methods.note: Clay uses API keys presented as bearer tokens in the Authorization header (Authorization: Bearer <API_KEY>) per the API Keys help article. The Webhooks & API article also shows Authorization: Bearer usage for REST endpoints.

credential_access.note: The signup page provides a self-serve account creation flow and the API Keys help article documents creating API keys in the dashboard. The help docs do not state that keys require sales or admin approval.

api_surface.note: The Webhooks and API help article documents a REST API under https://api.clay.com/v1/ with CRUD endpoints for tables and rows (GET /v1/tables, POST /v1/tables/{id}/rows, PATCH /v1/tables/{id}/rows/{row_id}, etc.), indicating broad coverage of core product objects (tables, rows).

buildability.note: Public REST API docs and self-serve API key generation exist in the help center; a developer can sign up and obtain keys to start integrating immediately.
